content.text: Both changes are done. Let me verify the final state of the files.

Both files look correct. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`generate-gifs.ts`** — Auth check moved before the flow loop (lines 76-91) into a non-recording browser context. The old auth check inside the `try` block (which was being recorded on video) has been removed. Each flow now goes straight to `flow.run()`.

**`flows/dashboard.ts`** — Added `waitForSelector("text=Total Checkpoints", { timeout: 15000 })` after navigation (line 15) to ensure the dashboard stat cards are actually visible before proceeding with the recording.

To verify, run:
```bash
cd app && GIF_ONLY=dashboard-overview npm run gif:generate
```

content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md — docs/
     2→
     3→Mintlify documentation site for partio, deployed to https://docs.partio.io.
     4→
     5→## Project overview
     6→
     7→This repo contains the documentation for the [partio CLI](https://github.com/partio-io/cli). Partio captures AI agent sessions alongside Git commits, preserving the reasoning behind code changes.
     8→
     9→## File structure
    10→
    11→```
    12→mint.json                  # Site config: navigation, tabs, colors, links
    13→introduction.mdx           # Home page
    14→quickstart.mdx             # Quick start guide
    15→core-concepts.mdx          # Sessions, checkpoints, strategies, git worktrees
    16→cli/
    17→  installation.mdx         # Install instructions (Homebrew, Go, source)
    18→  commands.mdx             # CLI command reference
    19→  configuration.mdx        # Config files, env vars
    20→  strategies.mdx           # Capture strategies
    21→app/
    22→  overview.mdx             # Dashboard overview (stats, heatmap, recent commits)
    23→  repositories.mdx         # Repository browser (search, filter, list)
    24→  repository-detail.mdx    # Per-repo tabs (Overview, Checkpoints, Commits, PRs, Code)
    25→  checkpoint-detail.mdx    # Checkpoint deep dive (Sessions, Plan, Files)
    26→  settings.mdx             # Theme customization (50+ OMARCHY themes)
    27→  self-hosting.mdx         # Self-hosting guide (env vars, install, production)
    28→integrations/
    29→  claude-code.mdx          # Claude Code integration guide
    30→images/
    31→  app/                     # Auto-generated GIFs for app documentation
    32→logo/                      # Logo SVGs for dark/light themes
    33→favicon.svg                # Site favicon
    34→```
    35→
    36→## MDX frontmatter
    37→
    38→Every `.mdx` page requires:
    39→
    40→```yaml
    41→---
    42→title: Page Title
    43→description: "Short description for SEO and navigation"
    44→---
    45→```
    46→
    47→## Mintlify components used
    48→
    49→- `<Tabs>` / `<Tab>` — tabbed content (install instructions, shell completions)
    50→- `<Card>` / `<CardGroup>` — linked cards for navigation
    51→- `<Warning>` — callout blocks
    52→- Markdown tables for structured data
    53→
    54→## Navigation
    55→
    56→Configured in `mint.json`:
    57→
    58→- **Tabs** define top-level sections (`CLI Reference`, `App`, `Integrations`)
    59→- **navigation** arrays define page ordering within each tab/group
    60→- Pages are referenced by their file path without extension (e.g., `cli/installation`)
    61→
    62→## Adding a new page
    63→
    64→1. Create a `.mdx` file with `title` and `description` frontmatter
    65→2. Add the file path (without `.mdx`) to the appropriate `navigation` group in `mint.json`
    66→
    67→## Local development
    68→
    69→```bash
    70→npm i -g mintlify
    71→mintlify dev          # Start local preview at localhost:3000
    72→```
    73→
    74→## Related repos
    75→
    76→| Repo | Description |
    77→|------|-------------|
    78→| [partio-io/cli](https://github.com/partio-io/cli) | The partio CLI (Go) |
    79→| [partio-io/site](https://github.com/partio-io/site) | Marketing website (Next.js) |
    80→

1→# Partio Documentation
     2→
     3→Documentation site for [partio](https://github.com/partio-io/cli), an open-source CLI that captures AI agent sessions alongside Git commits.
     4→
     5→**Live site:** [docs.partio.io](https://docs.partio.io)
     6→
     7→## Tech stack
     8→
     9→Built with [Mintlify](https://mintlify.com). Pages are written in MDX.
    10→
    11→## Local development
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→npm i -g mintlify
    15→mintlify dev
    16→```
    17→
    18→This starts a local preview at `http://localhost:3000`.
    19→
    20→## Deployment
    21→
    22→Deployments are automatic via Mintlify's GitHub integration. Pushing to `main` triggers a build and deploy to [docs.partio.io](https://docs.partio.io).
    23→
    24→## Adding a new page
    25→
    26→1. Create a `.mdx` file with frontmatter (`title` and `description`)
    27→2. Add the page path to the appropriate `navigation` group in `mint.json`
    28→
    29→## File structure
    30→
    31→```
    32→mint.json                  # Site config: navigation, colors, links
    33→introduction.mdx           # Home / introduction page
    34→quickstart.mdx             # Quick start guide
    35→core-concepts.mdx          # Sessions, checkpoints, strategies, worktrees
    36→cli/
    37→  installation.mdx         # Install instructions
    38→  commands.mdx             # CLI command reference
    39→  configuration.mdx        # Config files and env vars
    40→  strategies.mdx           # Capture strategies
    41→integrations/
    42→  claude-code.mdx          # Claude Code integration guide
    43→logo/                      # Logo assets (dark.svg, light.svg)
    44→favicon.svg                # Site favicon
    45→```
    46→
    47→## Related repos
    48→
    49→| Repo | Description |
    50→|------|-------------|
    51→| [partio-io/cli](https://github.com/partio-io/cli) | The partio CLI |
    52→| [partio-io/site](https://github.com/partio-io/site) | Marketing website (partio.io) |
    53→

1→# partio marketing website
     2→
     3→Marketing site for [partio](https://github.com/partio-io/cli) — an open-source CLI that captures AI agent sessions alongside Git commits, so you always know _why_ every line was written.
     4→
     5→## Tech stack
     6→
     7→- [Next.js 16](https://nextjs.org/) (App Router)
     8→- [React 19](https://react.dev/)
     9→- [TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)
    10→- [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/)
    11→- [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/)
    12→
    13→## Prerequisites
    14→
    15→- Node.js 18+
    16→- npm
    17→
    18→## Getting started
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→# Install dependencies
    22→npm install
    23→
    24→# Start the dev server
    25→npm run dev
    26→```
    27→
    28→Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) to view the site.
    29→
    30→### Build for production
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→npm run build
    34→npm start
    35→```
    36→
    37→## Project structure
    38→
    39→```
    40→site/
    41→├── public/                  # Static assets (logo SVG)
    42→├── src/
    43→│   ├── app/                 # Next.js App Router (layout + single page)
    44→│   │   ├── favicon.ico
    45→│   │   ├── globals.css      # Tailwind imports & CSS custom properties
    46→│   │   ├── layout.tsx       # Root layout (fonts, metadata)
    47→│   │   └── page.tsx         # Home page
    48→│   ├── components/
    49→│   │   ├── layout/          # Navbar, Footer
    50→│   │   ├── sections/        # Hero, Features, HowItWorks, CliDemo, OpenSource
    51→│   │   └── ui/              # Button, Badge, Terminal, FeatureCard
    52→│   └── lib/
    53→│       └── utils.ts         # Utilities (cn class-merge helper)
    54→├── eslint.config.mjs
    55→├── next.config.ts
    56→├── postcss.config.mjs
    57→├── tsconfig.json
    58→└── package.json
    59→```
    60→
    61→## Available scripts
    62→
    63→| Script          | Description                  |
    64→| --------------- | ---------------------------- |
    65→| `npm run dev`   | Start dev server             |
    66→| `npm run build` | Production build             |
    67→| `npm start`     | Serve production build       |
    68→| `npm run lint`  | Run ESLint                   |
    69→
    70→## Styling
    71→
    72→The site uses Tailwind CSS v4 with a dark-only theme defined via CSS custom properties in `src/app/globals.css`. Key color tokens:
    73→
    74→- `--background`, `--foreground` — base colors
    75→- `--accent`, `--accent-light`, `--accent-dark` — indigo brand accent
    76→- `--surface`, `--surface-light` — card/section backgrounds
    77→- `--border`, `--muted` — borders and secondary text
    78→- `--success`, `--warning` — status colors
    79→
    80→Fonts are Inter (sans) and JetBrains Mono (mono), loaded via `next/font/google`.
    81→
    82→## Related projects
    83→
    84→- [partio CLI](https://github.com/partio-io/cli) — the core CLI tool
    85→- [partio docs](https://docs.partio.io) — documentation site
    86→
    87→## License
    88→
    89→MIT
    90→

1→# CLAUDE.md — site/
     2→
     3→Marketing website for partio, an open-source CLI that captures AI agent sessions alongside Git commits.
     4→
     5→## Tech stack
     6→
     7→- Next.js 16 (App Router)
     8→- React 19
     9→- TypeScript
    10→- Tailwind CSS v4
    11→- Framer Motion 12
    12→
    13→## Commands
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→npm run dev    # Start dev server on localhost:3000
    17→npm run build  # Production build
    18→npm run lint   # Run ESLint
    19→```
    20→
    21→## Architecture
    22→
    23→- **App Router** — single-page app with `src/app/layout.tsx` (root layout) and `src/app/page.tsx` (home)
    24→- **Components** are organized into three directories:
    25→  - `src/components/layout/` — Navbar, Footer
    26→  - `src/components/sections/` — Hero, Features, HowItWorks, CliDemo, OpenSource
    27→  - `src/components/ui/` — reusable primitives (Button, Badge, Terminal, FeatureCard)
    28→- **Lib** — `src/lib/utils.ts` contains the `cn()` class-merge helper (clsx)
    29→- **Path alias** — `@/*` maps to `./src/*`
    30→
    31→## Conventions
    32→
    33→- **Client components** — section components, Navbar, FeatureCard, and Terminal use `"use client"` because they depend on Framer Motion. The root layout, Footer, Badge, and Button are server components.
    34→- **Styling** — Tailwind CSS v4 utility classes only. Theme colors are defined as CSS custom properties in `src/app/globals.css` and registered via `@theme inline`. Use the semantic tokens (e.g. `bg-background`, `text-accent`, `border-border`) rather than raw hex values.
    35→- **Animations** — Framer Motion with `motion.div`, `useInView`, and staggered variants. Follow the existing animation patterns when adding new sections.
    36→- **Class merging** — use `cn()` from `@/lib/utils` to merge Tailwind classes.
    37→- **TypeScript** — interfaces are defined inline in component files, not in separate type files.
    38→- **State** — no state management library. Use local `useState` where needed.
    39→- **Static content** — all copy is hardcoded in component files. There is no CMS or content layer.
    40→- **No API routes or server actions** — this is a purely static marketing site.
    41→- **No environment variables** — none are currently used.
    42→
    43→## External links
    44→
    45→- Docs: https://docs.partio.io
    46→- CLI repo: https://github.com/partio-io/cli
    47→

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information needed. Here's what I found:

## Running the Docs Site Locally

This project has **two separate sites**:

### 1. **Documentation Site** (`/docs` directory)
**Tech:** Mintlify

**Commands to run locally:**
```bash
npm i -g mintlify
mintlify dev
```

This starts a local preview at `http://localhost:3000`.

**Key files:**
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/docs/mint.json` — Site configuration
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/docs/README.md` — Documentation instructions
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/docs/CLAUDE.md` — Project overview

**Live site:** https://docs.partio.io

---

### 2. **Marketing Website** (`/site` directory)
**Tech:** Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4

**Commands to run locally:**
```bash
cd site
npm install
npm run dev
```

This starts a dev server at `http://localhost:3000`.

**Key files:**
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/site/package.json` — Dependencies and scripts
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/site/README.md` — Setup instructions
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-partio-docs/site/CLAUDE.md` — Architecture and conventions

---

**Summary:** If you're looking to run the **documentation site**, use Mintlify. If you're working on the **marketing website**, use Next.js.

Perfect! I have all the information needed. Here's what I found:

## Running the Docs Site Locally

This project has **two separate sites**:

### 1. **Documentation Site** (`/docs` directory)
**Tech:** Mintlify

**Commands to run locally:**
```bash
npm i -g mintlify
mintlify dev
```

This starts a local preview at `http://localhost:3000`.

---

### 2. **Marketing Website** (`/site` directory)
**Tech:** Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4

The docs site uses **Mintlify**. To run it locally:

```bash
cd docs
mintlify dev
```

This starts a preview at `http://localhost:3000`. You may need to install the CLI first with `npm i -g mintlify` if you haven't already.

message.content:
➜  docs git:(partio-docs) ✗ mintlify dev
zsh: command not found: mintlify

content.text:
```bash
npm i -g mintlify
```

Then retry:

```bash
cd docs && mintlify dev
```

message.content:
➜  docs git:(partio-docs) ✗ mintlify dev
error mint dev is not supported on node versions 25+. Please downgrade to an LTS node version.

content.text:
You need to use an LTS Node version. The quickest way if you have `nvm` or `fnm`:

```bash
nvm install 22
nvm use 22
mintlify dev
```

Or with `fnm`:

```bash
fnm install 22
fnm use 22
mintlify dev
```

If you don't have a Node version manager installed, I'd recommend installing `fnm`:

```bash
brew install fnm
fnm install 22
fnm use 22
mintlify dev
```

                          choose a diff algorithm
    --anchored <text>     generate diff using the "anchored diff" algorithm
    --word-diff[=<mode>]  show word diff, using <mode> to delimit changed words
    --word-diff-regex <regex>
                          use <regex> to decide what a word is
    --color-words[=<regex>]

    --[no-]relative[=<prefix>]

    -a, --[no-]text       treat all files as text
    -R                    swap two inputs, reverse the diff
    --[no-]exit-code      exit with 1 if there were differences, 0 otherwise

    --[no-]textconv       run external text conversion filters when comparing binary files

                          specify how differences in submodules are shown

                          hide 'git add -N' entries from the index
    --ita-visible-in-index
                          treat 'git add -N' entries as real in the index
    -S <string>           look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified string
    -G <regex>            look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified regex
    --pickaxe-all         show all changes in the changeset with -S or -G
    --pickaxe-regex       treat <string> in -S as extended POSIX regular expression
    -O <file>             control the order in which files appear in the output
    --rotate-to <path>    show the change in the specified path first
    --skip-to <path>      skip the output to the specified path
    --find-object <object-id>
                          look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified object
    --diff-filter [(A|C|D|M|R|T|U|X|B)...[*]]
                          select files by diff type
    --max-depth <depth>   maximum tree depth to recurse
    --output <file>       output to a specific file

content.text:
Here's a commit message for these changes:

```
Add Playwright-based GIF generation for app documentation

Automated screen recordings of key app flows (dashboard, repositories,
repo detail, checkpoint, settings) converted to GIFs for docs. Auth
check runs in a non-recording context to avoid capturing the login
screen, and the dashboard flow waits for stat card content before
recording.
```
